revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:平群神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Heguri Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Heguri-jinja, torii-1.jpg|280px]]
Torii and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|qid=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District, Nara|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara|1=Heguri, Nara}} Nishimiya 617 |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 20.9 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 14.3 |ISO = JP-29 |deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Major 5 seats)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |established = Unknown |annual_festival = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|qid=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} |alternate_names = Nishimiya |map = Japan Nara }} '''Heguri Shrine''' (平群神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nishimiya, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|qid=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District, Nara|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine is colloquially known as "Nishimiya." == Deities == The shrine currently enshrines one deityOn-site explanation board.: * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} According to the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, compiled in {{ill|927|en|927|qid=Q30470|lt=927|1=927}}, five deities are listed for Heguri Shrine. The original enshrined deities are believed to be ancestral gods of the {{ill|Heguri clan|ja|平群氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri clan|1=Heguri Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483498}}{{Harvid|Heguri Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. It is suggested that one or two deities were originally worshipped, with others added later{{Harvid|Heguri Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. There is another {{ill|Heguri Shrine (Kuwana)|ja|平群神社 (桑名市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri Shrine (Kuwana)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kuwana, Mie|en|Kuwana, Mie|qid=Q496472|lt=Kuwana, Mie|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, also a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It enshrines {{ill|Heguri_no_Tsuku|en|Heguri_no_Tsuku|qid=Q17216122|lt=Heguri no Tsuku|1=Heguri no Tsuku}}, the ancestor of the Heguri clan. The auxiliary shrine (摂社) to the left of the main shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}. == History == The founding date of Heguri Shrine is unknown. In the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, compiled in {{ill|927|en|927|qid=Q30470|lt=927|1=927}}, Heguri Shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under "Heguri Shrine, Five Seats, All Major, Monthly and Niiname Festivals," designating it as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} eligible to receive offerings during imperial rituals{{Harvid|Heguri Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. While the shrine was later identified as a major shrine, no definitive records confirm this{{Harvid|Heguri Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The shrine's location aligns with the historical center of the {{ill|Heguri District|ja|平群郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri District|1=Heguri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483501}} as described in the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyō Ruijushō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和名類聚抄}}{{Harvid|Heguri Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the 1441 (Kakitsu 1) {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|Kōfuku-ji|qid=Q1070863|lt=Kōfuku-ji|1=Kōfuku-ji}} record "Kanmu-chōsho" refers to "Heguri Daimyōjin," detailing the shrine's ancestral deity and shrine priests{{Harvid|Heguri Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Daimyōjin"{{Harvid|Heguri Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The temple Ryūkabō Nishimiya Mitsu-ji was part of the shrine complex but was dissolved during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} reforms. Its site is now used as the shrine office{{Harvid|Heguri Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Heguri Shrine was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Harvid|Heguri Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Major Festival == * Autumn Grand Festival: Held annually on {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|qid=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Heguri Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Heguri Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2392596}}|author=Ritatsu Ōya|year=2000|chapter=Heguri Shrine|title=Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Places 日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 4: 大和 <新装復刊版>|publisher={{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Places|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346470}}|isbn=978-4560025031|ref={{Harvid|Heguri Shrine (Kami)|2000}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Heguri-jinja}} * {{ill|Heguri clan|ja|平群氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri clan|1=Heguri Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483498}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]] {{ill|Heguri Shrine|ja|平群神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri Shrine|qq=}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January {{translated page|ja|平群神社|version=100306097|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:平群神社]] {{Commons category|Heguri-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q97220447}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Heguri clan]] [[Category:Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
